name: synthesize
purpose: Cross-source synthesis across all analyzed sources (repos, websites, documents, media) — produces emergent themes, ecosystem gaps, reading chains, mental model evolution, fit portfolio, knowledge map, and interactive opportunity matrix. Consolidates /repo-synthesis + /website-synthesis.
notes: Version 2.0 (2026-04-16 — Session #284, 109 decisions). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 21557b + REFERENCE.md 27975b). Total: ~49.5KB. Consumer skill — reads handler outputs (analysis.json, creator-view.md, value-map.json), does NOT re-analyze. Replaces /repo-synthesis and /website-synthesis (both deprecated, redirect). REFERENCE.md is comprehensive (§1-§14: per-paradigm reshape rules, section spec, handler contracts, output contracts, anti-patterns, integration). 4 synthesis paradigms: thematic (default), narrative, matrix, meta-pattern. Phase 2.5 and 4.5 invoke /convergence-loop (quick preset). Subagent hybrid for 10+ sources (max 10 per agent). State file: .claude/state/synthesize.state.json (v2 schema). Opportunities ledger at .research/analysis/synthesis/opportunities-ledger.jsonl — cross-run lifecycle tracking. Inbound callers: /analyze (--synthesize flag triggers it), /recall, 4 CAS handler skills, /session-end, /skill-audit. JASON-OS portability blocked: entire CAS pipeline (SQLite, rebuild-index.js, Zod schema, extraction-journal.jsonl) does not exist in JASON-OS.
deferred_sections: self-audit.js Phase 4 dimensional extensions (Dim 11 contradiction, Dim 12 drift — referenced but deferred scaffold)
agent_types_spawned: subagent (general-purpose, max 10 sources per pass in Phase 2 for 10+ source corpus)

name: pr-review
purpose: Process external PR code review feedback (CodeRabbit, Qodo, SonarCloud, Gemini) through an 8-step protocol — parses all items, categorizes by severity and origin, fixes in priority order, tracks deferred items to TDMS, and captures learnings.
dependencies: npx tsx write-invocation.ts (Step 6 JSONL write + invocation tracking)
external_refs: gh CLI (PR size check, stale HEAD verification)
sanitize_fields: CodeRabbit + Gemini reviewer sources (dropped in JASON-OS port)
docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md path (SoNash markdown learning log — JASON-OS uses simpler .planning/PR_REVIEW_LEARNINGS.md)
notes: Version 4.6 (SoNash) — JASON-OS port is v4.6-jasonos-v0.1 (ported 2026-04-17). File count: 7 files (SKILL.md 23422b + ARCHIVE.md 7944b + reference/ subdir with 5 MDs: LEARNING_CAPTURE.md, PARALLEL_AGENT_STRATEGY.md, PRE_CHECKS.md, SONARCLOUD_ENRICHMENT.md, TDMS_INTEGRATION.md). Total: ~49.4KB. PORTED to JASON-OS with deliberate scope reduction: CodeRabbit + Gemini dropped, pipeline-style learning capture dropped, TDMS replaced with /add-debt stub, neighbor refs trimmed. JASON-OS port has reference/ subdir with only 2 files (PRE_CHECKS, PARALLEL_AGENT_STRATEGY) — 3 SoNash-specific reference files excluded. Step 2 DAS framework (Signal/Dependency/Risk scoring 0-6) is highly portable. Security Threat Model checklist (Step 0) is universal. State file: .claude/state/task-pr-review-{pr}-r{round}.state.json. D26 backward flow (Step 1 retro pattern check) depends on retros.jsonl — sanitize or stub.
active_scripts: cd scripts/reviews && npx tsc && node dist/write-review-record.js
port_lineage_frontmatter: SoNash `pr-review` v4.6 → JASON-OS v0.1 trimmed port

name: doc-optimizer
purpose: Wave-based orchestrator deploying 13 parallel agents across 5 waves to analyze, auto-fix formatting/headers/links, report issues as JSONL, and generate actionable improvement recommendations for every non-archived document in the repository.
external_refs: wc (line counting for agent output verification)
sanitize_fields: scripts/check-doc-headers.js through scripts/check-doc-placement.js (SoNash-specific doc validation scripts)
scripts/debt/intake-audit.js + TDMS pipeline (SoNash TDMS — not in JASON-OS)
notes: Version 1.5 (2026-04-03). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 16817b + prompts.md 29987b). Total: ~46.8KB. prompts.md contains all 13 agent prompt specifications (extracted in v1.4, 71% SKILL.md size reduction). 5-wave structure: Wave 0 (discovery) → Wave 1 (independent analysis: 3 parallel + 1 sequential) → Wave 2 (graph-dependent: 4 parallel) → Wave 3 (synthesis: 2 parallel) → Wave 4 (enhancement: 3 parallel) → Orchestrator unification. Context safety protocol embedded: CRITICAL RETURN PROTOCOL (agents return only completion signal, not full results), Windows 0-byte output fallback (anthropics/claude-code#39791). Step 9 TDMS intake = not-portable (hardcoded MASTER_DEBT.jsonl copy + generate-views.js). Doc validation scripts (check-doc-headers.js etc.) are SoNash-specific but concept is universally portable — JASON-OS port would need replacement scripts or script stubs. 13 agents: 1A FORMAT+LINT (AUTO-FIX), 1B HEADER+METADATA (AUTO-FIX), 1C EXTERNAL-LINKS (REPORT), 1D CONTENT-ACCURACY (MIXED), 2A-2D (MIXED/REPORT), 3A-3B (REPORT), 4A-4C (REPORT).

Convergence loops N/A (router decisions are deterministic). Handoff Contract v1.2 — standardized payload {target, auto_detected_type, flags}. JASON-OS portability blocked: CAS pipeline (SQLite, cas/ scripts, Zod schema, extraction-journal.jsonl) does not exist in JASON-OS. The routing logic itself is universally portable.

name: pr-retro
purpose: PR Review Retrospective — actionable analysis of a PR's review cycle with convergence-loop deliverable verification, interactive findings walkthrough, dashboard for missing retros, and mandatory action item implementation.
dependencies: pr-review skill (upstream — produces reviews.jsonl consumed here)
alerts skill (Step 9 cross-skill if hook abuse found)
external_refs: gh CLI (merged PR list, commit verification via gh pr view N --json commits)
sanitize_fields: reviews.jsonl path (.claude/state/ — SoNash JSONL data store)
notes: Version 4.8 (2026-03-18). File count: 3 files (SKILL.md 15034b + REFERENCE.md 27966b + ARCHIVE.md 2696b). Total: ~45.7KB. REFERENCE.md is large (27966b) — contains claim extraction sources, CL result processing, finding presentation templates, verification criteria, JSONL record schemas, batch retro scope, data enrichment details. ARCHIVE.md holds pre-v4 history. Dashboard mode reads reviews.jsonl + retros.jsonl — both are SoNash-specific JSONL data stores. Deliverable verification (Step 1) MUST use gh pr view N --json commits for commit listing (NOT git log ranges — known false-positive risk from Session #264). Semantic pattern matching required for recurrence detection (exact-match fails). D26 backward flow: Step 1 reads retros.jsonl action_items[], flags repeat patterns, auto-elevates to MAJOR. Action item implementation is BLOCKING (Critical Rule #10). PR companion to /pr-review — tightly coupled via reviews.jsonl/retros.jsonl data contracts. NOT ported to JASON-OS (Foundation scope excludes retro).

name: comprehensive-ecosystem-audit
purpose: Orchestrates all 8 ecosystem audits (hook, session, TDMS, PR, health, skill, doc, script) in 2 staged waves with 4-concurrent-agent limit, aggregates results into a unified health report with cross-audit insights and priority-ranked findings.
notes: Version 1.1 (2026-02-24 trim; v1.2 adds health audit 2026-03-10). File count: 4 files (SKILL.md 7517b + reference/ subdir: AGGREGATION_GUIDE.md 9857b + RECOVERY_PROCEDURES.md 9432b + WAVE_DETAILS.md 12793b). Total: ~39.6KB. portability=not-portable because all 8 sub-audits are either not-portable (tdms-ecosystem-audit) or sanitize-then-portable — a full port requires ALL 8 sub-audit skills to land first. SKILL.md is deliberately compact (thin orchestrator shell) — all detail in reference/ subdir (3 files). Uses _shared/ecosystem-audit/ pattern indirectly (sub-audits read those modules, orchestrator only calls their run scripts). CRITICAL RETURN PROTOCOL: each agent saves JSON to .claude/tmp/ecosystem-{name}-result.json and returns ONLY grade/score/error counts — never reads full output into context. Stage 1: 5 parallel agents. Stage 2: 3 parallel agents. Stage 3: sequential aggregation with weighted grading formula. Cross-audit analysis: domain heat map, shared file hotspots, common pattern violations, top 20 findings. Result files and progress JSON deleted on completion. Inbound: systematic-debugging references it in When NOT to Use. It orchestrates D1a skills (hook, tdms, pr, session, script, health, skill, doc ecosystem-audits) — hub aggregator.
agent_types_spawned: subagent (one per ecosystem audit — 8 total across 2 stages)

name: create-audit
purpose: Interactive 7-phase wizard for creating any type of audit (code, process, infrastructure, ecosystem, or custom) — uses multi-agent discovery with convergence loop and deep-plan Q&A to produce audit skills, multi-AI templates, and TDMS-ready output through approval-gated workflow.
dependencies: skill-audit skill (Phase 5 validation gate — MUST run on generated audit)
skill-creator skill (routing — non-audit skills use that instead)
deep-research skill (Phase 1 domain research check, optional)
sanitize_fields: scripts/debt/ pipeline scripts (SoNash TDMS — not in JASON-OS)
TDMS integration requirement (generated audits MUST use 4 TDMS scripts)
notes: Version 3.0 (2026-03-16 — 32 decisions, 75→88 target quality score). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 18322b + REFERENCE.md 20280b). Total: ~38.6KB. REFERENCE.md contains: Guard Rails, Anti-Patterns, Integration, Artifact Contracts, Compaction Resilience details, generation templates per audit type, question categories for deep-plan Q&A. 7-phase wizard: Warm-Up → Context → Discovery → Planning+Approval → Build → Validate → Self-Audit → Closure. Phase 2 convergence loop MANDATORY (Critical Rule 1). Evidence-based self-audit with grep-based proof (Phase 6). Audit type taxonomy: (1) single-session multi-agent, (2) ecosystem diagnostic, (3) interactive review, (4) custom hybrid. Generated audits MUST use 4 TDMS scripts — this is the primary not-portable constraint. The Q&A patterns, convergence loop, approval gate, and code generation templates are universally portable concepts. State file: .claude/state/task-create-audit-{name}.state.json. SoNash-specific: invocation tracking, TDMS pipeline, audit-specific output dirs. JASON-OS port would need TDMS replaced with /add-debt equivalent.

name: audit-process

dependencies: docs/technical-debt/MASTER_DEBT.jsonl (Stage 7 cross-reference, mandatory before interactive review)
sanitize_fields: Firebase Cloud Functions scope (types 12-14 — SoNash product)
npm scripts scope (60 scripts in package.json — SoNash-specific count)
notes: Version 2.5 (2026-04-03). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 13907b + prompts.md 23588b). Total: ~37.5KB. prompts.md contains all 22 agent prompt specifications. portability=not-portable because: (1) audit scope types 12-15 are Firebase/MCP — SoNash product, (2) TDMS pipeline hardcoded, (3) counts (~29 hooks, ~49 skills, ~60 npm scripts, ~61 standalone scripts) are SoNash-specific. The 7-stage orchestration pattern, Windows 0-byte fallback, MASTER_DEBT cross-reference gate, interactive review process, and evidence requirements are all highly portable patterns. 22 agents across 6 stages: Stage 1 (6 Explore — inventory), Stage 2 (3 Explore — redundancy), Stage 3 (4 mixed — effectiveness), Stage 4 (3 mixed — performance), Stage 5 (3 mixed — quality), Stage 6 (3 mixed — improvements), Stage 7 (1 sequential — synthesis). State file: .claude/state/audit-process-<date>.state.json. References _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md — standard audit procedures shared module. episodic memory search at Step 0 = SoNash-specific MCP.

name: multi-ai-audit
purpose: Single-entry-point orchestrator for multi-AI consensus audits — supports any-format AI input across 9 categories (code, security, performance, refactoring, documentation, process, engineering-productivity, enhancements, ai-optimization), aggregates with schema normalization, and automates TDMS intake with roadmap track assignment.
external_refs: External AI systems (CodeRabbit, Gemini, Qodo, SonarCloud, custom — user pastes findings)
sanitize_fields: state file path (.claude/multi-ai-audit/session-state.json — note: uses .claude/multi-ai-audit/ not .claude/state/ — unusual)
notes: Version 1.5 (2026-02-16). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 9564b + templates.md 26272b). Total: ~35.8KB. templates.md contains Phases 2-9 detail (template code blocks, JSONL schema examples, phase instructions). 9 audit categories: code, security, performance, refactoring, documentation, process, engineering-productivity, enhancements, ai-optimization. Workflow state machine: session init → category select → template output → collect findings (paste) → aggregate → unify → interactive review → TDMS intake → roadmap integration → complete. Schema normalization/fixing (fix-schema.js) handles messy AI output formats. CANON IDs generated per aggregated finding. State file uses .claude/multi-ai-audit/ directory (not .claude/state/) — unusual path. Categorized as not-portable because: (1) TDMS pipeline deeply embedded (Phases 7-8), (2) scripts/multi-ai/ processing pipeline is SoNash-specific, (3) ai-optimization category specifically audits SoNash's token waste/skill overlap patterns. The multi-AI consensus workflow concept and schema normalization approach are highly portable patterns.

name: audit-agent-quality
purpose: Hybrid agent quality audit — automated structural checks and interactive behavioral review using audit-team (reviewer + fixer) for all agent definitions in .claude/agents/ — produces per-agent scores (0-100), ecosystem grade (A-F), JSONL findings for TDMS, and trend tracking across 13 quality categories.
dependencies: skill-audit skill (routing — agent quality vs skill quality)
skill-ecosystem-audit skill (Domain 5 — agent orchestration wiring neighbor)
sanitize_fields: CLAUDE.md Section 7 trigger coverage gaps check (Stage 1C — CLAUDE.md structure is portable but SoNash-specific trigger table)
GSD agents boundary (SoNash GSD framework — not in JASON-OS)
notes: Version 1.2 (2026-03-17 — 27 decisions in v1.1, Stage 4 + Stage 2.5 + Categories 12-13 added in v1.2). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 17893b + REFERENCE.md 16215b). Total: ~34.1KB. REFERENCE.md contains category weights and scoring formula, agent prompt templates, artifact contract table. 4-stage hybrid architecture: Stage 1 (3 parallel Explore agents with internal 3-pass CL each + 1 cross-agent CL verification), Stage 2 (audit-team: reviewer + fixer, sequential per-agent), Stage 2.5 (batch implementation + behavioral testing), Stage 3 (synthesis + cross-stage CL), Stage 4 (built-in agent optimization). Critical team-leader pattern: Explore agents return JSONL as text (cannot write files) — orchestrator writes to disk. 13 quality categories. GSD agents are flag-only (D#6 — audit but don't modify upstream agents). Value filter rule (D#7): propose improvements only if measurably better output quality. Converges 3 times: within each Stage 1 agent (3-pass internal), every 6 agents in Stage 2 (mid-audit CL), and Stage 3 cross-stage CL. State file: .claude/state/task-audit-agent-quality.state.json.

name: recall
purpose: Query layer of the Content Analysis System (CAS) knowledge base — SQLite-backed FTS5 search over extraction candidates, filterable by tags, type, source, and sort order. Serves /analyze pipeline consumers.

sanitize_fields: scripts/cas/ scripts (full CAS pipeline — not in JASON-OS)
notes: Version 1.2 (2026-04-17 — Session #285 skill-audit pass). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 12122b + REFERENCE.md 19577b). Total: ~31.7KB. REFERENCE.md §§1-4 cover FTS5 syntax, result presentation formats (extractions, sources, stats), follow-up options, index rebuild, corpus validation. portability=not-portable because the entire CAS backend (SQLite, recall.js, rebuild-index.js, extraction-journal.jsonl, content-analysis.db) does not exist in JASON-OS. The skill is essentially a thin presentation layer over CAS. Self-audit script (scripts/skills/recall/self-audit.js) is explicitly DEFERRED — Phase 5 is a scaffold per SKILL.md. Tag display is category-aware: About (semantic_tags), Type (taxonomic_tags), Legacy tags (orphan_tags). OPDEP-1 = --target=sources standalone mode dependency. Inbound callers from /analyze (mentions /recall as next step), /synthesize (integration), /deep-plan and /brainstorm (downstream consumers mentioned in Integration). The CAS ecosystem (analyze + recall + synthesize) is a major SoNash-specific feature cluster.
deferred_sections: Phase 5 self-audit (scripts/skills/recall/self-audit.js — explicitly deferred per SKILL.md)

name: convergence-loop
purpose: Multi-pass verification of claims about reality using agent-based discovery, T20 tallies (Confirmed/Corrected/Extended/New), and composable behaviors — implements tenet T20 (research_convergence_loops) and T25 (discovery phases SHOULD use convergence loops).

skill-creator skill (programmatic caller — integration complete Session #222)

notes: Version 1.1 (2026-03-15 — S1-S22 skill-audit improvements). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 13745b + REFERENCE.md 16070b). Total: ~29.8KB. PORTED to JASON-OS as convergence-loop/ (confirmed — found in JASON-OS .claude/skills/). REFERENCE.md covers behavior definitions + agent prompt templates, domain slicing templates, required output format, state schema, customization hook. portability=portable because: no external scripts, no SoNash-specific state paths, pure behavioral protocol. JASON-OS port retains compatibility: agentskills-v1, metadata.version: 1.1. Hub skill: 8+ inbound callers documented (analyze, brainstorm, debt-runner, deep-plan, deep-research, pr-retro, skill-creator, synthesize). Hard cap: 5 passes (configurable). State file: .claude/state/convergence-loop-{topic}.state.json. Programmatic mode documented for skills that integrate without invoking /convergence-loop directly. name: github-health
purpose: GitHub platform health assessment with 7 phases (security, actions, deps, config, release, insights, PR health), per-finding triage (Fix/Defer/Skip/Suppress), scoped single-phase mode, session-begin quick check integration, and inline fix execution.
dependencies: alerts skill (downstream — reads github-health-history.jsonl for Agent Quality category)
gh-fix-ci skill (delegation target for Actions/Release/PR phases)
scripts/run-github-health.js (quick mode script — consumed by session-begin hook)
external_refs: gh CLI (all phase data collection + fix execution)
sanitize_fields: session-begin hook coupling (uses github-health quick check in hook)
notes: Version 1.1 (2026-04-04 — 19 decisions applied, 1.0 initial). File count: 2 files (SKILL.md 13600b + REFERENCE.md 15332b). Total: ~28.9KB. REFERENCE.md contains full check lists, API endpoints, fix recipes per phase (including UI-only fix instructions with URLs), grading rules, license flag rules. Phase 0 pre-flight: token scope check (gh auth status), dedup guard (<30min), warm-up context. Per-finding triage with 4 options: Fix/Defer/Skip/Suppress. Suppression has expiry dates + P0 suppression warning. Decision #24: fixes create github-health-fixes branch, atomic commits, push with user approval. Fix branch PR created after all fixes. UI-only fixes (Decision #23): step-by-step layman instructions. Decision #12: license flags (WARNING: copyleft + unknown; INFO: FSL; Ignore: MIT, Apache, BSD). Quick mode (--quick): script only, used by session-begin hook (no triage). REFERENCE.md Recipe 14 has UI-only fix instructions. 28 decisions total (see .planning/github-health-skill/DECISIONS.md). Has research artifact at .research/github-health/RESEARCH_OUTPUT.md. NOT ported to JASON-OS. Portability note: core assessment logic + triage flow are portable — gh CLI is universal. gh-fix-ci delegation would need alternative.
